exec_summary: This research paper explores the intersection of physics and theology, specifically how the Second Law of Thermodynamics supports theological concepts of grace. It argues that just as physical systems inevitably move towards disorder without external intervention, spiritual coherence or alignment with divine principles requires an outside source—grace—to maintain order and prevent decay. This insight underscores the necessity of external support for both physical and spiritual systems to sustain themselves against natural tendencies toward disarray.
plain_english: Imagine we're talking about how things naturally tend to fall apart over time, like a sandcastle slowly being washed away by the tide. This idea comes from something called the Second Law of Thermodyn
